criteria: Python syntax lint from write_file passed

claim_table.claim: Hermes controller system is Hermes-owned rather than Claude/Codex clone
criteria: README states imported lessons are constraints
README names Hermes-native route fields and surfaces

claim_table.claim: Hermes controller system is canonical/default runtime
criteria: explicitly blocked until fresh runtime integration smoke and audit

evidence_boundary: This scenario proves the v0 harness exists, selftests, and produces route-truth receipts with explicit blockers. It does not prove Hermes default runtime adoption, model-worker quality, or autonomous long-run behavior.

claude: Policy text is insufficient if async/background completions render as raw logs.
Measurement datasets need comparable schemas and claim parity before promotion.
Full scaffold must regenerate after background completions, not just initial turns.
codex: Worker reports are receipts to audit, not truth by themselves.
hermes: Use Hermes route fields and proof-depth ceilings.
Use profile-scoped memory/skills/wiki surfaces rather than another runtime's files.
Keep Wizard v4.3 scaffold compact and non-log-shaped while preserving route truth.

routes.evidence_boundary: Proves the boundary/design surface exists on disk; does not prove default Hermes runtime admission.
Proves harness selftest ran and rejected an intentionally bad overclaim scenario; does not prove all future scenarios are meaningful.
Blocks any claim that this harness is canonical/default runtime.
routes.receipt: blocked: no fresh Hermes CLI/gateway integration smoke has been run for this v0 harness

routes.evidence_boundary: Lists next bounded tests only; does not execute them.
